title: ODI Matrix — Museums, Historical Sites, and Similar Institutions

sections.title: Satisfaction Recalibration
sections.content:
Incumbent mix for NAICS 712110 used to ground-truth JTBD initial scores:

| Alternative | Share | Monitoring | Remote Alert | Audit Log | Heritage-Safe | Key Strengths | Key Weaknesses |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|---|---|
| A1 Basic mechanical alarm | 45% | None | No | No | Yes | Low cost, no power needed | Physical patrol required; no detection lag below hours |
| A2 Wired alarm panel | 25% | Panel | Yes | Yes | No | Remote alert, BMS integration | 3–12 month heritage consent barrier; high retrofit cost |
| A3 Türwächter IoT (Türwächter) | 20% | Dashboard | Yes | Yes | Yes | Battery-powered retrofit; no consent needed | Proprietary EasyWave protocol; 30m range; €730/door |
| A4 Gallery attendant / guard | ~15%* | Human | No | Partial | Yes | Flexible response | Linear cost; coverage gaps; fatigue |
| A5 Do nothing | 10% | None | No | No | Yes | Zero P1 burden | Compliance risk; zero detection |

\* A4 share is approximate; overlaps with A1/A3 deployments.

**Recalibration result:** JTBD initial scores align with incumbent evidence. Key confirmations:
- E8 sat=3 confirmed: A1 (45%) requires physical patrol; after-hours vault detection is near-zero without IoT or guard.
- E11 sat=3 confirmed: No alternative except A3 (20% share) provides centralized single-interface monitoring.
- E9 sat=8 confirmed: All alternatives maintain single-motion escape by regulatory design — satisfaction is structurally high.
- E6 sat=6 confirmed: A1 (45%) is surface-mount and partially heritage-safe; A2 (25%) requires invasive wiring — market average ~6.

No adjustments to initial JTBD importance scores. No adjustments needed to satisfaction scores — incumbent data confirms them.

---

sections.title: ODI Entry Matrix
sections.content:
Formula: `opportunity_score = importance + max(importance − satisfaction, 0)`
Underserved: `imp > 7.0 AND sat < 5.0` | Overserved: `imp < 3.0 AND sat > 7.0`

### Market-Level Needs (Step 06 — E1–E19)

| # | Error Statement | Step | Imp | Sat | OS | U/O | p_rel | Impact |
|---|---|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|---|
| E1 | Minimize the likelihood of high-value collection storage area exits remaining unmonitored due to incomplete upfront coverage planning, leaving ≥1 after-hours exit event undetected per quarter | DEFINE | 8 | 4 | **12** | U | false | safety |
| E2 | Increase the ability to segment emergency exit monitoring alerts by zone risk level (storage vault vs. public gallery vs. service corridor) so that a single after-hours activation of a vault exit triggers immediate escalation | DEFINE | 8 | 3 | **13** | U | false | safety |
| E3 | Increase the ability to pre-validate wireless signal coverage through heritage-grade masonry walls (stone, brick, reinforced concrete) before committing to gateway placement and device purchase | LOCATE | 7 | 4 | **10** | — | true | reliability |
| E4 | Minimize the likelihood of RF signal loss between exit devices and the gateway caused by thick historic masonry walls, creating silent monitoring gaps exceeding 30 minutes | LOCATE | 8 | 5 | **11** | — | true | reliability |
| E5 | Minimize the time required to install and commission emergency exit monitoring across a 20-room museum without drilling into historic fabric — achieving full coverage in <1 day | PREPARE | 8 | 5 | **11** | — | true | time |
| E6 | Minimize the likelihood of installation fixing methods leaving permanent marks or penetrations in listed building fabric, causing non-compliance with heritage conservation officer requirements | PREPARE | 9 | 6 | **12** | — | false | safety |
| E7 | Increase the ability to verify that every installed exit device transmits correctly through actual building materials and that alarm operation is unimpeded — within a single commissioning walk-through that the heritage officer can witness | CONFIRM | 8 | 5 | **11** | — | false | reliability |
| E8 | Minimize the likelihood of an unauthorized emergency exit activation in a storage vault or conservation area remaining undetected for >2 minutes during closed hours | EXECUTE | 10 | 3 | **17** | U | false | safety |
| E9 | Minimize the likelihood of an emergency exit monitoring device adding any resistance or additional step to the escape path that could delay evacuation of visitors with reduced mobility | EXECUTE | 10 | 8 | **12** | — | false | safety |
| E10 | Minimize the likelihood of legitimate authorized uses (staff exiting at end of shift, conservation team accessing storage) generating false alerts that erode duty manager confidence in the monitoring system | EXECUTE | 7 | 4 | **10** | — | false | reliability |
| E11 | Increase the ability of a single duty security officer or remote monitoring team to track real-time exit status across all monitored doors from one centralized interface without physical patrol — during closed-hours coverage windows | MONITOR | 9 | 3 | **15** | U | false | time |
| E12 | Minimize the time from emergency exit activation event to security officer notification to <60 seconds during after-hours monitoring periods | MONITOR | 9 | 4 | **14** | U | false | time |
| E13 | Minimize the likelihood of wireless connectivity loss to any monitored exit device creating a silent gap exceeding 15 minutes — particularly during after-hours periods when no staff are on-site | MONITOR | 8 | 5 | **11** | — | true | reliability |
| E14 | Minimize the time to reset a triggered exit monitoring device and restore it to active monitoring state after an incident investigation — without requiring specialist tools not available to on-duty museum staff | MODIFY | 7 | 6 | **8** | — | false | time |
| E15 | Minimize the likelihood of a high-priority storage area activation being deprioritized due to alert fatigue from higher-frequency low-risk public gallery exit events | MODIFY | 8 | 5 | **11** | — | false | safety |
| E16 | Increase the ability to produce a complete, timestamped digital log of all emergency exit activations and response actions formatted for submission to the fire authority, insurer, and heritage funder — without manual compilation | CONCLUDE | 8 | 3 | **13** | U | false | safety |
| E17 | Minimize the time required to identify and proactively replace batteries approaching depletion threshold across a deployment of ≥10 exit monitoring devices before monitoring capability is lost — accounting for reduced battery performance in climate-controlled conservation environments | CONCLUDE | 7 | 4 | **10** | — | true | reliability |
| E18 | Minimize the variability in emergency exit monitoring coverage status across the building at any time — ensuring no exits enter an unmonitored state due to technical failure without triggering a staff notification | P2 Core | 9 | 4 | **14** | U | false | safety |
| E19 | Minimize the likelihood of emergency exit compliance deficiencies being cited during fire marshal inspection or heritage authority review — resulting in temporary closure of gallery spaces or loss of grant-funding eligibility | P2 Core | 9 | 5 | **13** | — | false | safety |

### Product-Constraint Needs (Step 10 — N1–N5)

Injected with `job_step_number=0`, `job_step_name="product_constraint"`, `product_related=true`.

| # | Error Statement | Source Constraint | Imp | Sat | OS | U/O | Impact |
|---|---|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|---|
| N1 | Minimize the likelihood of 868 MHz signal degradation from heritage-grade masonry (stone walls >400mm, reinforced concrete) creating silent monitoring gaps on emergency exit devices exceeding 15 minutes — in buildings where signal loss is not visually apparent | C3 Wireless Range | 8 | 4 | **12** | U | reliability |
| N2 | Minimize the time required to pre-validate 868 MHz radio coverage through actual building wall materials before committing to gateway placement — preventing post-installation discovery of exit zones with insufficient signal strength | C3 Wireless Range | 7 | 4 | **10** | — | reliability |
| N3 | Minimize the likelihood of 9V block battery depletion in exit monitoring devices below operational threshold within the 2-year maintenance window — accounting for reduced electrochemical performance in climate-controlled conservation environments (15–20°C, 45–55% RH) | C4 Battery Life | 7 | 4 | **10** | — | reliability |
| N4 | Minimize the time required to achieve complete emergency exit monitoring coverage across all doors in a multi-room heritage building using exclusively surface-mount fixing methods — completing installation within a single operational day without disrupting gallery activities | C5 Surface Mount | 8 | 5 | **11** | — | reliability |
| N5 | Minimize the likelihood of EasyWave protocol incompatibility causing integration failure when the museum BMS or intruder detection panel uses BACnet, KNX, or IP-based protocols — requiring additional unplanned gateway hardware to achieve centralized monitoring | C6 Protocol Lock-In | 6 | 5 | **7** | — | reliability |

---

sections.title: Market ODI Summary
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Market** | Museums, Historical Sites, and Similar Institutions |
| **NAICS** | 712110 |
| **Total needs scored** | 24 |
| **Underserved (imp > 7 AND sat < 5)** | 8 |
| **Overserved (imp < 3 AND sat > 7)** | 0 |
| **Avg opportunity score** | 11.58 |
| **Overall zone** | Appropriately Served → borderline Underserved |

**Top 5 Opportunities (by descending OS):**

| Rank | OS | Statement |
|:---:|:---:|---|
| 1 | 17 | Minimize the likelihood of an unauthorized emergency exit activation in a storage vault or conservation area remaining undetected for >2 minutes during closed hours (E8) |
| 2 | 15 | Increase the ability of a single duty security officer or remote monitoring team to track real-time exit status across all monitored doors from one centralized interface without physical patrol (E11) |
| 3 | 14 | Minimize the time from emergency exit activation event to security officer notification to <60 seconds during after-hours monitoring periods (E12) |
| 4 | 14 | Minimize the variability in emergency exit monitoring coverage status across the building at any time — ensuring no exits enter an unmonitored state without staff notification (E18) |
| 5 | 13 | Increase the ability to segment emergency exit monitoring alerts by zone risk level (storage vault vs. public gallery vs. service corridor) so that vault activation triggers immediate escalation (E2) |

---

sections.title: Key Findings
sections.content:
| # | Finding |
|---|---|
| 1 | **E8 (OS=17) is the dominant opportunity** — vault activation undetected >2 min. A1 (45% share) provides zero remote detection; A5 (10%) provides zero detection. The IoT dashboard + EasyWave radio are the only incumbent that addresses this directly. Directly validated by FIT (Battery-Powered Operation = 6.67, highest feature fit). |
| 2 | **Monitoring cluster drives value** — E11+E12+E18 (OS 15/14/14) form a tight trio: single-officer overview, sub-60-sec alert, continuous coverage consistency. All addressed by A3 alone. Together they define the core IoT ROI case. |
| 3 | **8 underserved needs, 0 overserved** — market is structurally under-served. No incumbent over-delivers on any dimension. A1 (45%) leaves detection, monitoring, and audit entirely unaddressed. |
| 4 | **E9 (OS=12) is appropriately served despite high importance** — sat=8 because all alternatives maintain single-motion escape by regulatory design (DIN EN 179/1125). This is a qualifying threshold, not a differentiator. |
| 5 | **Protocol lock-in (N5, OS=7) is the lowest opportunity** — imp=6, sat=5. The EasyWave constraint is a friction point for S1/S5 BMS-integrated segments but does not rise to an urgent unmet need at the market level. Confirmed by FIT: Wireless BUS Interface scored inferior (vendor=5, incumbent=7). |
| 6 | **ODI–Feature Fit cross-check: no flag** — ODI avg (11.58) is below the 12-threshold for "high opportunity / low feature fit" warning. Feature Fit avg (5.72) is below the 7-threshold for "low opportunity / high feature fit" warning. No inconsistency flag required. |

---

rows.Key Weaknesses: Physical patrol required; no detection lag below hours
3–12 month heritage consent barrier; high retrofit cost

rows.Error Statement: Minimize the likelihood of high-value collection storage area exits remaining unmonitored due to incomplete upfront coverage planning, leaving ≥1 after-hours exit event undetected per quarter

Increase the ability to segment emergency exit monitoring alerts by zone risk level (storage vault vs. public gallery vs. service corridor) so that a single after-hours activation of a vault exit triggers immediate escalation
Increase the ability to pre-validate wireless signal coverage through heritage-grade masonry walls (stone, brick, reinforced concrete) before committing to gateway placement and device purchase
Minimize the likelihood of RF signal loss between exit devices and the gateway caused by thick historic masonry walls, creating silent monitoring gaps exceeding 30 minutes
Minimize the time required to install and commission emergency exit monitoring across a 20-room museum without drilling into historic fabric — achieving full coverage in <1 day
Minimize the likelihood of installation fixing methods leaving permanent marks or penetrations in listed building fabric, causing non-compliance with heritage conservation officer requirements

Increase the ability to verify that every installed exit device transmits correctly through actual building materials and that alarm operation is unimpeded — within a single commissioning walk-through that the heritage officer can witness
Minimize the likelihood of an unauthorized emergency exit activation in a storage vault or conservation area remaining undetected for >2 minutes during closed hours
Minimize the likelihood of an emergency exit monitoring device adding any resistance or additional step to the escape path that could delay evacuation of visitors with reduced mobility

Minimize the likelihood of legitimate authorized uses (staff exiting at end of shift, conservation team accessing storage) generating false alerts that erode duty manager confidence in the monitoring system

Increase the ability of a single duty security officer or remote monitoring team to track real-time exit status across all monitored doors from one centralized interface without physical patrol — during closed-hours coverage windows
Minimize the time from emergency exit activation event to security officer notification to <60 seconds during after-hours monitoring periods
Minimize the likelihood of wireless connectivity loss to any monitored exit device creating a silent gap exceeding 15 minutes — particularly during after-hours periods when no staff are on-site

Minimize the time to reset a triggered exit monitoring device and restore it to active monitoring state after an incident investigation — without requiring specialist tools not available to on-duty museum staff
Minimize the likelihood of a high-priority storage area activation being deprioritized due to alert fatigue from higher-frequency low-risk public gallery exit events

Increase the ability to produce a complete, timestamped digital log of all emergency exit activations and response actions formatted for submission to the fire authority, insurer, and heritage funder — without manual compilation

Minimize the time required to identify and proactively replace batteries approaching depletion threshold across a deployment of ≥10 exit monitoring devices before monitoring capability is lost — accounting for reduced battery performance in climate-controlled conservation environments

Minimize the variability in emergency exit monitoring coverage status across the building at any time — ensuring no exits enter an unmonitored state due to technical failure without triggering a staff notification

Minimize the likelihood of emergency exit compliance deficiencies being cited during fire marshal inspection or heritage authority review — resulting in temporary closure of gallery spaces or loss of grant-funding eligibility

Minimize the likelihood of 868 MHz signal degradation from heritage-grade masonry (stone walls >400mm, reinforced concrete) creating silent monitoring gaps on emergency exit devices exceeding 15 minutes — in buildings where signal loss is not visually apparent

Minimize the time required to pre-validate 868 MHz radio coverage through actual building wall materials before committing to gateway placement — preventing post-installation discovery of exit zones with insufficient signal strength

Minimize the likelihood of 9V block battery depletion in exit monitoring devices below operational threshold within the 2-year maintenance window — accounting for reduced electrochemical performance in climate-controlled conservation environments (15–20°C, 45–55% RH)

Minimize the time required to achieve complete emergency exit monitoring coverage across all doors in a multi-room heritage building using exclusively surface-mount fixing methods — completing installation within a single operational day without disrupting gallery activities

Minimize the likelihood of EasyWave protocol incompatibility causing integration failure when the museum BMS or intruder detection panel uses BACnet, KNX, or IP-based protocols — requiring additional unplanned gateway hardware to achieve centralized monitoring

rows.Value: Museums, Historical Sites, and Similar Institutions

rows.Statement: Minimize the likelihood of an unauthorized emergency exit activation in a storage vault or conservation area remaining undetected for >2 minutes during closed hours (E8)
Increase the ability of a single duty security officer or remote monitoring team to track real-time exit status across all monitored doors from one centralized interface without physical patrol (E11)
Minimize the time from emergency exit activation event to security officer notification to <60 seconds during after-hours monitoring periods (E12)
Minimize the variability in emergency exit monitoring coverage status across the building at any time — ensuring no exits enter an unmonitored state without staff notification (E18)
Increase the ability to segment emergency exit monitoring alerts by zone risk level (storage vault vs. public gallery vs. service corridor) so that vault activation triggers immediate escalation (E2)

rows.Finding: **E8 (OS=17) is the dominant opportunity** — vault activation undetected >2 min. A1 (45% share) provides zero remote detection; A5 (10%) provides zero detection. The IoT dashboard + EasyWave radio are the only incumbent that addresses this directly. Directly validated by FIT (Battery-Powered Operation = 6.67, highest feature fit).

**Monitoring cluster drives value** — E11+E12+E18 (OS 15/14/14) form a tight trio: single-officer overview, sub-60-sec alert, continuous coverage consistency. All addressed by A3 alone. Together they define the core IoT ROI case.
**8 underserved needs, 0 overserved** — market is structurally under-served. No incumbent over-delivers on any dimension. A1 (45%) leaves detection, monitoring, and audit entirely unaddressed.

**E9 (OS=12) is appropriately served despite high importance** — sat=8 because all alternatives maintain single-motion escape by regulatory design (DIN EN 179/1125). This is a qualifying threshold, not a differentiator.

**Protocol lock-in (N5, OS=7) is the lowest opportunity** — imp=6, sat=5. The EasyWave constraint is a friction point for S1/S5 BMS-integrated segments but does not rise to an urgent unmet need at the market level. Confirmed by FIT: Wireless BUS Interface scored inferior (vendor=5, incumbent=7).

**ODI–Feature Fit cross-check: no flag** — ODI avg (11.58) is below the 12-threshold for "high opportunity / low feature fit" warning. Feature Fit avg (5.72) is below the 7-threshold for "low opportunity / high feature fit" warning. No inconsistency flag required.
